questions.prompt: ThreatLocker policies inherit automatically from a parent organisation down to its children.
questions.explanation: There's no implicit policy inheritance. Cross-tenant propagation is via the Copy Policies operation; a policy in the parent doesn't take effect on children unless explicitly copied (and deployed).

questions.prompt: An MSP tech leaves the company. Which is the most reliable cleanup move?
choices: Disable their account at the top MSP organisation
Disable the account at the MSP layer AND audit child organisations to remove any direct grants
Change all customer policies that mention them
Wait for the next quarterly review
questions.explanation: MSP-layer access is the main door, but customers can also have direct user grants (especially in older onboardings). A clean offboarding audits child orgs as well as disabling at the top, otherwise stale rights linger.
